spread over a long period. When the given birth time presents
the very end or the beginning of a sign, it perplexes not only
the average student of astrology, but also the scholar. Hence
there is need to know how to rectify the time ofbirth.

2
Traditional Methods
Our astrological texts have indicated some principles which
are said to enable us to rectify the time of birth.
I. In the Brihaijataka (4.11) Varahamihira observed:

When powerful lagna, Sun, Moon and Guru occupy odd
signs or navamsas, there is a masculine birth. If these occupy
even sings and amsas, they give rise to female birth. If the
Sun and Guru are in odd signs, there is a male birth. If the
Moon, Kuja and Shukra are in even signs a female is born. If
these planets are in common signs aspected by Budha, there
will be twins whose sex is determined by the nature of the
stgns.
